creative_brief: Clean, editorial layout with a split-hero: left side shows a close-up of hands holding fresh produce at a farm gate, right side shows the Brookvale hub interior — real, unpolished. Below the fold, use simple iconography for the 4-point investment case. Muted greens and warm neutrals. No stock photography. Typography-forward with plenty of white space to let the copy breathe.
headline: You already fund the grocery industry. Why not own part of one?
subhead: FarmThru is a farm-direct grocery store cutting out unnecessary middlemen — and we're letting everyday customers become owners through equity crowdfunding. A $5 refundable deposit reserves your priority access.
hero_copy:
Every week, you hand money to a supply chain that moves food 2,400km before it reaches you. Farmers get squeezed. You pay the markup. And nobody asks if you'd rather have a seat at the table.

FarmThru is a different kind of grocery store. We buy direct from farms across NSW, and customers collect from our Brookvale hub — no unnecessary middlemen inflating the price. Now we're opening ownership to the people who actually eat the food.

This isn't charity. It's an investment thesis: a leaner supply chain, lower overhead, and customers who buy because they believe in what they own.

sections.heading: The model: why fewer steps means a better store
sections.body:
Supermarkets run food through warehouses, distributors, and logistics networks that span the country. Each layer adds cost. Each layer adds time. By the time broccoli hits the shelf, it's travelled further than most people drive in a month.

FarmThru works differently. We partner with roughly 15–20 farms and suppliers across NSW. Customers order online, then collect from our Brookvale hub Monday to Friday. No massive retail lease. No aisle after aisle of stock expiring under fluorescent lights.

The result: fresher food, fairer prices for farmers, and a cost structure that doesn't depend on charging you more to cover waste. Ordering is on-demand — no subscriptions, no lock-in, no membership fees. You order when you want.

sections.heading: The investment case — in plain language
sections.body:
Here's what we think makes FarmThru worth backing:

1. A shorter supply chain means lower cost of goods. Farm to hub to customer. That's it.
2. Hub-and-collect keeps overhead low. No fleet of delivery vans. No prime retail rent.
3. Bulk buying rewards commitment. Customers save more when they buy more — which means higher average order values.
4. Every customer-owner has a reason to come back. Ownership creates loyalty no points card can match.

Minimum investment is $50. Maximum is $10,000 per investor. The campaign launches on Birchal, Australia's leading equity crowdfunding platform.

But here's what we won't tell you: that this is guaranteed to work. It's not. Early-stage investing carries real risk, including the possibility of losing your entire investment. We believe in this model — but belief isn't a balance sheet. Read the offer document carefully before deciding.

sections.heading: What your $5 refundable deposit gets you
sections.body:
Reserve your spot on the VIP waitlist and you'll get:

— First access to the equity crowdfunding campaign before it opens to the public
— Priority updates as we build toward launch
— Exclusive Q&A sessions with the founding team
— A fully refundable deposit — no commitment, no risk to look closer

You're not buying shares yet. You're securing your place in line so you can make an informed decision when the campaign goes live. If you change your mind, you get your $5 back. No questions.

sections.heading: Who this is for
sections.body:
This isn't for people chasing quick returns. It's for people who spend real money on groceries every week and wonder why the system doesn't work better.

People who'd rather know the farm their food came from. People who think a grocery store should answer to its customers, not just its shareholders. People who want to be part of building something — not just buying from it.

If that sounds like you, put down $5 and see what we're building. Collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday. Own a piece of where your food comes from.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru's equity crowdfunding campaign will be hosted on Birchal. Investing in early-stage companies carries significant risk, including the risk of losing your entire investment. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. This page is not financial advice. Any investment decision should be made after reading the offer document in full. FarmThru does not guarantee returns or project future financial performance. The $5 VIP deposit is fully refundable and does not constitute an investment or obligation to invest.
